gdprSections.heading: 2. Purpose and Legal Basis of Processing

elements.text: We process personal data exclusively for the following purposes:
Legal basis:
Art. 6 (1) (b) GDPR (performance of a contract or pre-contractual measures)
Where data processing is required for organizational or technical reasons, it is additionally based on
Art. 6 (1) (f) GDPR (legitimate interest in proper business operations).
If you contact us by e-mail, phone or form, the data you provide (e.g. name, contact details, message) will be processed solely to handle your request.
The data will be deleted once it is no longer required for the purpose of processing and no statutory retention obligations apply.
Online appointment bookings are handled via Salonized.
Personal data (e.g. name, contact details, appointment and service information) is processed to manage and perform appointments.
Legal basis:
Art. 6 (1) (b) GDPR
Salonized processes personal data as an independent provider and/or on our behalf. Salonized's privacy policy applies in addition.
We use Jotform to provide forms (e.g. enquiries, consent forms).
Data entered into forms is transmitted to Jotform and processed there for technical provision of the form service.
Legal basis:
Art. 6 (1) (b) GDPR

Processing outside the European Union (e.g. USA) cannot be excluded. In such cases, data transfers are based on appropriate safeguards pursuant to Art. 44 et seq. GDPR (e.g. EU-US Data Privacy Framework and/or Standard Contractual Clauses).
We operate a profile on Instagram, a service provided by Meta Platforms Ireland Ltd., 4 Grand Canal Square, Grand Canal Harbour, Dublin 2, Ireland.
We also use Instagram Ads to promote our services.

When visiting our Instagram profile or interacting with our ads, Meta processes personal data (e.g. usage data, interactions, technical information). We have no influence on the scope or manner of this processing.
Processing is based on
Art. 6 (1) (f) GDPR (legitimate interest in public relations and advertising).
Meta provides us with anonymized statistics (Insights). For this processing, there is joint controllership pursuant to Art. 26 GDPR. The agreement is available at:
A transfer of data to third countries (e.g. USA) cannot be excluded. Meta relies on appropriate safeguards pursuant to Art. 44 et seq. GDPR.
Further information is available in Instagram's privacy policy:
Personal data is disclosed only if:
No data is disclosed for external tracking or advertising purposes beyond the platforms mentioned above.
Personal data is stored only for as long as necessary for the stated purposes or as required by statutory retention obligations.
You have the following rights under the GDPR at any time:

items: Right to restriction of processing (Art. 18 GDPR)
Right to withdraw consent with effect for the future (Art. 7 (3) GDPR)

elements.text: Requests may be sent to:
You have the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority. The competent authority for Berlin is:
Berlin Commissioner for Data Protection and Freedom of Information
Alt-Moabit 59–61, 10555 Berlin
E-mail: mailbox@datenschutz-berlin.de
This privacy policy applies in its current version.

sections.heading: § 1 Scope of Application
paragraphs: These General Terms and Conditions apply to all contracts between theSchneider.hair, owner Dennis Schneider, Gärtnerstraße 31, 10245 Berlin, Germany (hereinafter referred to as "the Salon") and its clients.
They apply to all services in the field of hairdressing, cosmetic treatments, hair extensions and hair thickening, as well as to the sale of hair care and styling products.
Any differing terms and conditions of the client shall not apply unless expressly agreed in writing.

sections.heading: § 2 Appointment Scheduling
paragraphs: Appointments become binding once they have been agreed upon in person, by phone or electronically.
The Salon endeavors to keep agreed appointment times. Minor delays do not entitle the client to any claims for compensation.
If the client arrives more than 10 minutes late, the Salon may shorten the service or cancel the appointment if proper performance is no longer possible for organizational reasons.

sections.heading: § 3 Cancellations and No-Show Fee
paragraphs: Appointments are binding once confirmed.
Cancellations are free of charge if made at least 48 hours before the scheduled appointment.
In the event of cancellation less than 48 hours before the appointment, the Salon is entitled to charge a cancellation fee of 50% of the agreed service price.
In the event of unexcused failure to attend the appointment, the Salon may charge 100% of the agreed service price as a no-show fee.
The client expressly reserves the right to prove that no damage or a lesser amount of damage has occurred.

sections.heading: § 4 Deposits
paragraphs: For certain services, especially time-intensive or material-intensive treatments, the Salon may require a reasonable deposit.
Deposits will be credited against the final invoice if the appointment takes place.
If the appointment is canceled in due time in accordance with § 3 (2), the deposit will be refunded in full.
In the case of late cancellation or no-show, the deposit may be offset against the cancellation or no-show fee.

sections.heading: § 5 Hair Orders (Extensions)
paragraphs: Hair orders (e.g. extensions) are custom-made products tailored specifically to the client.
By placing the order, the client agrees bindingly to the purchase. Color, length, quantity and quality are agreed upon in advance.
There is no right of withdrawal for custom-made products in accordance with Section 312g (2) No. 1 of the German Civil Code (BGB).
Ownership of the ordered hair transfers to the client upon placement of the order.
An appointment for installation must be scheduled within two months from the date of ordering.
If the client fails to attend the installation appointment, the cancellation and no-show provisions shall apply accordingly.

sections.heading: § 6 Prices and Payment
paragraphs: The prices valid at the time of service provision according to the current price list apply.
All prices include the applicable statutory value-added tax (VAT).
The total amount is due immediately after the service has been rendered.
Payment can be made in cash or by EC / debit card, unless otherwise agreed.

sections.heading: § 7 Default of Payment
paragraphs: In the event of payment default, the statutory provisions of German law (§§ 286 et seq. BGB) shall apply.
Any fees incurred due to chargebacks caused by the client (e.g. card payments) will be passed on to the client.
The client reserves the right to prove that no damage or a lesser amount of damage has occurred.

sections.heading: § 8 Liability
paragraphs: The Salon shall be liable only for damages caused by intent or gross negligence.
In cases of slight negligence, the Salon shall only be liable for the breach of essential contractual obligations and limited to the foreseeable, typical damage.
Liability for damages resulting from injury to life, body or health remains unaffected.
The Salon assumes no liability for clothing, bags, luggage or valuables brought onto the premises.
The Salon is not liable for damage to clothing or personal items caused by the client's own fault or failure to follow instructions.
For chemical or cosmetic treatments expressly requested by the client, the Salon assumes no liability for individual intolerances or allergic reactions, unless caused by intent or gross negligence.
No liability shall arise in cases of force majeure or circumstances beyond the Salon's control.

sections.heading: § 9 Warranty and Rectification
paragraphs: Complaints must be reported to the Salon without undue delay after becoming aware of them.
The client is initially entitled to rectification (subsequent performance).
Warranty claims do not apply to damage caused by improper care, unsuitable products or interventions by third parties.
Natural wear and tear does not constitute a defect.

sections.heading: § 10 Retention of Title
paragraphs: Any goods sold shall remain the property of the Salon until full payment has been made.

sections.heading: § 11 Confidentiality
paragraphs: All persons working in the Salon are obliged to maintain confidentiality regarding personal and business-related matters of clients.

sections.heading: § 12 Data Protection
paragraphs: Personal data is processed exclusively in accordance with applicable data protection laws, in particular the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).
Further details can be found in the separate privacy policy.

sections.heading: § 13 Governing Law and Jurisdiction
paragraphs: German law shall apply.
The place of jurisdiction shall be Berlin, insofar as legally permissible.

sections.heading: § 14 Severability Clause
paragraphs: Should individual provisions of these General Terms and Conditions be or become invalid, the validity of the remaining provisions shall remain unaffected.
